vulnerability.title: Disable Accepting ICMP Redirects for All IPv6 Interfaces
vulnerability.description: To set the runtime status of the net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects kernel parameter, run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects=0 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then # Comment out any occurrences of net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects from /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf files for f in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do # skip systemd-sysctl symlink (/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf -> /etc/sysctl.conf) if [[ "$(readlink -f "$f")" == "/etc/sysctl.conf" ]]; then continue; fi matching_list=$(grep -P '^(?!#).*[\s]*net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects.*$' $f | uniq ) if ! test -z "$matching_list"; then while IFS= read -r entry; do escaped_entry=$(sed -e 's|/|\\/|g' <<< "$entry") # comment out "net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects" matches to preserve user data sed -i --follow-symlinks "s/^${escaped_entry}$/# &/g" $f done <<< "$matching_list" fi d

triage_decision.reason: Disabling acceptance of ICMP redirects for all IPv6 interfaces prevents potential spoofing or redirection attacks that could be used to bypass network security controls.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to output a JSON array of objects with command, path, content, mode.
We must fix the finding: Disable Accepting ICMP Redirects for All IPv6 Interfaces. The remediation is to set net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects to 0.

We need to set kernel parameter net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0. We can use sysctl -w.

We need to make it persistent via file in /etc/sysctl.d: net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0.

Thus we need two commands perhaps: one to run sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects=0 (to set runtime), and one to write file /etc/sysctl.d/net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects.conf with content "net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0". However they said Use run_cmd for EXACTLY ONE shell command at a time. Do NOT chain with && ; or multiline scripts. So we must provide exactly ONE command. We cannot combine setting sysctl and writing file in one command? Could we use a single command that does both? For example using a bash -c "sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects=0 && echo 'net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0' > /etc/sysctl.d/net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects.conf". That's chaining with && which is not allowed. So we must choose only one action.

Which action is required? The fix description: To set the runtime status of the kernel parameter, run the sysctl command. To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the line to a file in /etc/sysctl.d. Both are needed for full remediation. But maybe they consider that setting runtime is enough? However they also mention persistence. The instruction says "To set the runtime status ... run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects=0 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0". So both are needed.

But we have constraint: Use run_cmd for EXACTLY ONE shell command at a time. Do NOT chain with && ; or multiline scripts. This suggests we cannot use &&. So we need to decide which single command to use. Perhaps we can use a command that writes the file and also sets sysctl via a single command like using "sysctl -p -w"? Not sure. There's no direct way to both set and persist in one command unless we use a script file that does both. But that would be a script (multi-line) maybe not allowed.

Given typical remediation, they often expect two separate commands: one for runtime, one for persistence. However the instruction set says Use run_cmd for EXACTLY ONE shell command at a time. Could be that they expect we to provide two separate entries in the JSON array, each with its own command, path, content, mode. That would be two objects, each with one command. That satisfies "EXACTLY ONE shell command at a time" per object. So we can have two objects in the array, each with one command. That's allowed. So we will output two objects.

First object: run sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects=0. path: maybe not needed? The schema: command string, path string, content string, mode string (like "000444"?). For sysctl we don't need to write to a file, so path could be empty string? content empty? mode maybe not applicable? We'll need to guess. Usually for sysctl we don't need path or content. Mode maybe empty. We'll set path "" and content "" and mode "".

Second object: write file /etc/sysctl.d/net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects.conf with content "net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0". path: that file. mode: maybe "0644"? Typically file mode 0644 is rw-r--r--? Actually 0644 is octal for rw-r--r--? Let's compute: 0644 octal = 0*512 + 6*64 + 4*8 + 4 = 0+384+32+4=420 decimal. That's not typical. Usually we use "0644" for file mode? In many SCAP remediations they use mode "0644" for files. We'll follow that.

Thus output JSON array:

[
  {
    "command": "sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects=0",
    "path": "",
    "content": "",
    "mode": ""
  },
  {
    "command": "echo 'net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0' > /etc/sysctl.d/net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects.conf",
    "path": "/etc/sysctl.d/net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects.conf",
    "content": "net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0",
    "mode": "0644"
  }
]

But note the second command uses echo and redirection. That's a shell command. It's fine.

We must ensure proper JSON escaping. The command string includes single quotes. We need to escape them for JSON. So command: "echo 'net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0' > /etc/sysctl.d/net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects.conf". In JSON we need to escape the single quotes as \' . So command: "echo \'net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0\' > /etc/sysctl.d/net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects.conf". Also the path string includes slash etc. No need to escape.

Similarly content string: "net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0". No need to escape.

Now output.
